_comment: FAQ Schema Templates for each tool page. Paste directly into RankMath > Schema > FAQ schema builder, OR add via wp_head as JSON-LD. Replace yoursite.com with your actual domain.
_usage: RankMath Free supports FAQ schema via: Edit Page > RankMath > Schema > Add Schema > FAQ. Add each question/answer pair there. Alternatively, paste the JSON-LD block for each page into the page's HTML using a Custom HTML block in the block editor.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate my monthly loan payment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the amortization formula: M = P × [r(1+r)^n] / [(1+r)^n−1], where P is the principal, r is the monthly interest rate (annual rate ÷ 12 ÷ 100), and n is the number of monthly payments. Our free loan payment calculator applies this formula automatically.

mainEntity.name: What is a good monthly payment for a $25,000 loan?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a $25,000 loan at 6.5% APR over 5 years, the monthly payment is approximately $488.59. Lower rates and longer terms reduce monthly payments but increase total interest paid over the life of the loan.

mainEntity.name: Does this loan calculator work for mortgages?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Enter the loan amount (home price minus down payment), your mortgage APR, and loan term. Note: this shows principal + interest only. Your actual mortgage payment may also include property taxes, homeowner's insurance, and PMI.

mainEntity.name: How do I lower my monthly loan payment?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can lower your monthly payment by borrowing less, securing a lower interest rate (improve your credit score), extending your loan term, or making a larger down payment. Note that a longer term means paying more total interest.

mainEntity.name: How is credit card interest calculated?

acceptedAnswer.text: Credit card interest is calculated using your Daily Periodic Rate (DPR): DPR = APR ÷ 365. Your monthly interest charge = average daily balance × DPR × days in billing cycle. For example, a $1,000 balance at 24% APR: DPR = 0.0658%, monthly interest ≈ $20.

mainEntity.name: How long will it take to pay off my credit card?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on your balance, APR, and monthly payment. Paying only the minimum (often 1–2% of balance) can take decades. For a $3,000 balance at 20% APR, paying $100/month takes about 4 years and costs ~$1,800 in interest. Paying $200/month cuts it to under 18 months.

mainEntity.name: What is a good APR for a credit card?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of 2024, the average credit card APR in the U.S. is around 20–24%. Rates below 20% are considered good. Cards for excellent credit (750+) may offer 15–18% APR. The best strategy is to pay your full balance monthly to avoid interest entirely.

mainEntity.name: What is compound interest?

acceptedAnswer.text: Compound interest is interest calculated on both the initial principal AND accumulated interest from prior periods. Unlike simple interest, it causes exponential growth over time — often called 'interest on interest.'

mainEntity.name: How much will $10,000 grow in 10 years at 7%?

acceptedAnswer.text: At 7% annual interest compounded monthly, $10,000 grows to approximately $20,097 in 10 years — nearly doubling. This illustrates the Rule of 72: divide 72 by your annual rate to estimate doubling time. At 7%, 72 ÷ 7 ≈ 10.3 years.

mainEntity.name: What compounding frequency should I use?

acceptedAnswer.text: Choose the frequency that matches your actual account. Savings accounts typically compound daily. CDs may compound daily or monthly. Bonds typically compound semi-annually. For general investment projections, monthly or annual compounding is commonly used.

mainEntity.name: How do I convert my annual salary to hourly rate?

acceptedAnswer.text: Divide your annual salary by the number of hours worked per year. Standard formula: hourly = annual ÷ (hours/week × weeks/year). For a $60,000 salary at 40 hours/week, 52 weeks: $60,000 ÷ 2,080 = $28.85/hour.

mainEntity.name: How many working hours are in a year?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard full-time year has 2,080 working hours (40 hours/week × 52 weeks). Accounting for 10 federal holidays it drops to 2,000 hours. With 2 weeks vacation: 1,960 hours. Use 2,080 as the baseline unless your employer specifies otherwise.

mainEntity.name: What is $50,000 a year as an hourly wage?

acceptedAnswer.text: $50,000 per year ÷ 2,080 hours = approximately $24.04 per hour. Monthly: ~$4,167. Weekly: ~$961.54. Daily (8-hour day): ~$192.31.

mainEntity.name: How much should I tip at a restaurant?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the United States, 18–20% is standard for good service at sit-down restaurants. For exceptional service, 25% or more is appropriate. For counter service or takeout, 10–15% is common.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate a 20% tip quickly in my head?

acceptedAnswer.text: Move the decimal point one place left to find 10%, then double it for 20%. For a $75 bill: 10% = $7.50, so 20% = $15.00. For 15%, take 10% and add half: $7.50 + $3.75 = $11.25.

mainEntity.name: What is a tip on a $100 bill at 20%?

acceptedAnswer.text: 20% of $100 = $20 tip. Total bill = $120. Split among 4 people, each person pays $30 ($25 food + $5 tip per person).

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate a percentage of a number?

acceptedAnswer.text: Multiply the number by the percentage and divide by 100. Formula: result = (number × percentage) ÷ 100. Example: 15% of 200 = (200 × 15) ÷ 100 = 30.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate percentage change?

acceptedAnswer.text: Percentage change = ((new value − old value) ÷ old value) × 100. Example: price goes from $80 to $100: ((100 − 80) ÷ 80) × 100 = 25% increase. A negative result means a decrease.

mainEntity.name: What percentage is 30 out of 150?

acceptedAnswer.text: 30 out of 150 = (30 ÷ 150) × 100 = 20%. Use this formula (part ÷ whole × 100) whenever you need to find what percent one number is of another.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate a discounted price?

acceptedAnswer.text: Multiply the original price by (1 − discount% ÷ 100). Example: $120 item with 25% off: $120 × (1 − 0.25) = $120 × 0.75 = $90. Your savings = $120 − $90 = $30.

mainEntity.name: How much do I save with a 30% discount on a $200 item?

acceptedAnswer.text: 30% of $200 = $60 savings. Final price = $200 − $60 = $140. To verify: $140 ÷ $200 = 0.70, meaning you pay 70% of the original price.

mainEntity.name: How do I find the original price before a discount?

acceptedAnswer.text: Divide the sale price by (1 − discount% ÷ 100). Example: item is $75 after 40% off: original price = $75 ÷ (1 − 0.40) = $75 ÷ 0.60 = $125.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate fuel cost for a road trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: Formula: fuel cost = (distance ÷ MPG) × price per gallon. Example: 300-mile trip, 30 MPG car, gas at $3.50/gallon: (300 ÷ 30) × $3.50 = 10 gallons × $3.50 = $35 total fuel cost.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to drive 500 miles?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on your car's MPG and the gas price. At 25 MPG and $3.50/gallon: (500 ÷ 25) × $3.50 = 20 gallons × $3.50 = $70. At 35 MPG: (500 ÷ 35) × $3.50 ≈ $50. Better fuel economy means significant savings on long trips.

mainEntity.name: What is a good MPG for a car?

acceptedAnswer.text: For gas-powered vehicles, 30+ MPG combined is considered good. Compact cars typically get 30–40 MPG. SUVs average 20–28 MPG. Hybrids can achieve 45–55 MPG. EPA ratings are available at fueleconomy.gov for any vehicle.

mainEntity.name: What is PITI in a mortgage payment?

acceptedAnswer.text: PITI stands for Principal, Interest, Taxes, and Insurance — the four components of a full monthly mortgage payment. Principal reduces your loan balance, Interest is the lender's fee, Taxes are property taxes (often escrowed), and Insurance includes homeowner's insurance and PMI if applicable.

mainEntity.name: When does PMI go away?

acceptedAnswer.text: PMI (Private Mortgage Insurance) is automatically cancelled by lenders when your loan balance reaches 78% of the original home value (i.e., you have 22% equity), per the Homeowners Protection Act. You can also request cancellation at 80% LTV (20% equity). Our calculator shows the exact month PMI drops.

mainEntity.name: How much house can I afford?

acceptedAnswer.text: A common rule is the 28/36 rule: your monthly mortgage payment (PITI) should not exceed 28% of your gross monthly income, and total debt payments should not exceed 36%. For a $6,000/month income, max mortgage payment ≈ $1,680. Use our mortgage calculator to find a home price that fits your budget.

mainEntity.name: Should I make extra mortgage payments?

acceptedAnswer.text: Extra payments apply directly to principal, reducing total interest paid and shortening your loan term. Even $100/month extra on a $300,000 30-year mortgage at 7% saves over $40,000 in interest and cuts ~4 years off the loan. Our calculator shows exact savings in the Extra Payments tab.

mainEntity.name: How many miles is 1 kilometer?

acceptedAnswer.text: 1 kilometer = 0.621371 miles. As a quick estimate, multiply km by 0.6. For example, 10 km ≈ 6 miles (exact: 6.214 miles).

mainEntity.name: How do I convert Celsius to Fahrenheit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Formula: °F = (°C × 9/5) + 32. Example: 25°C = (25 × 1.8) + 32 = 77°F. Key reference: 0°C = 32°F, 100°C = 212°F, 37°C = 98.6°F (body temperature).

mainEntity.name: How many pounds is 1 kilogram?

acceptedAnswer.text: 1 kilogram = 2.20462 pounds. To convert quickly: multiply kg by 2.2. Example: 70 kg × 2.2 = 154 lbs (exact: 154.32 lbs).

mainEntity.name: How many km is a 5K race?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 5K race is exactly 5 kilometers = 3.107 miles. A 10K = 6.214 miles. A half marathon = 21.097 km = 13.109 miles. A full marathon = 42.195 km = 26.219 miles.
